mission.title: alertmanager: telegram_config.api_url should be optional
mission.description: telegram_config.api_url should be optional. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current alertmanager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your alertmanager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
helm list -n alertmanager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working alertmanager installation.

steps.title: Review alertmanager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant alertmanager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl get configmap -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=alertmanager
```
I just updated to alertmanager-0.24.0 and went to use the new Telegram integration.

steps.title: Apply the fix for telegram_config.api_url should be optional
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
alertmanager, version 0.24.0 (branch: HEAD, revision: f484b17fa3c583ed1b2c8bbcec20ba1db2aa5f11)
  build user:       root@45f97b18b623
  build date:       20220325-09:29:04
  go version:       go1.17.8
  platform:         linux/arm
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl get events -n alertmanager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "telegram_config.api_url should be optional" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the source issue for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager/issues/2876
codeSnippets:
alertmanager, version 0.24.0 (branch: HEAD, revision: f484b17fa3c583ed1b2c8bbcec20ba1db2aa5f11)
  build user:       root@45f97b18b623
  build date:       20220325-09:29:04
  go version:       go1.17.8
  platform:         linux/arm

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with alertmanager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
